steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: Look at $23x^3+2x^2+9x$. The coefficients $23$, $2$, and $9$ share no common factor other than $1$. Every term has at least one factor of $x$, so the GCF is just $x$.

steps.title: Compare with each choice
steps.content:
• A) $23x(x^2+2x+9)$: distributes to $23x^3+46x^2+207x$, not the original. ✗
  • B) $9x(23x^3+2x^2+1)$: distributes to $207x^4+18x^3+9x$, which has an $x^4$ term. ✗
  • C) $x(23x^2+2x+9)$: distributes to $23x^3+2x^2+9x$. ✓
  • D) $34(x^3+x^2+x)$: distributes to $34x^3+34x^2+34x$, wrong coefficients. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
